_comment: Expected outcomes for each fixture. 'decision' must match. 'must_mention' is a list of substrings the response/reasoning should ideally contain (case-insensitive); used as soft signal, not a hard fail. 'notes' is for the candidate, not checked.
_decision_values: pass         — strong candidate, advance to next round
fail         — clearly weak, send respectful rejection
needs_info   — incomplete application, ask for missing pieces
skipped      — not an application (marketing, recruiter outreach, gibberish, etc.)

strong_01_senior_fullstack.notes: Staff eng with massive shipped scope. Should be a clear pass.

strong_02_mid_strong_project.notes: Mid-level + real side product with paying users.

strong_03_nontraditional.notes: No CS degree but multiple shipped products. Watch for credentialism bias.

strong_04_junior_exceptional.notes: Junior but real product traction. Should not be auto-rejected for years of experience.

weak_01_buzzwords_no_ship.notes: Pure buzzword resume. Rejection should specifically say 'no evidence of shipped products' or similar.

weak_02_forks_only.notes: Trainee with only forks. Rejection should call out lack of original work.

weak_03_ai_generated_tells.notes: Vague metrics, AI-generated tone, all internal repos. A bonus if the agent flags the resume's tells.

weak_04_screenshot_portfolio.notes: No real engineering signal. Rejection should be respectful and specific.

borderline_01_resume_strong_github_dead.notes: Acceptable answers: pass with caveat, OR ask for code samples / a call. Auto-reject would be wrong.

borderline_02_github_strong_no_portfolio.notes: Strong open-source body of work. Lack of portfolio site shouldn't be disqualifying.

borderline_03_private_repos_only.notes: Reasonable to ask for code samples or take it on signal. Auto-reject = bad.

edge_01_no_attachment.notes: Should ask for the missing resume specifically.

edge_02_docx_not_pdf.notes: Should ask for a PDF version. Bonus: mention why (parsing reliability).

edge_03_scanned_pdf.notes: Acceptable: ask for a text-extractable PDF, OR run OCR and evaluate. Either is defensible.

edge_04_no_github.notes: Reasonable to ask for code samples or alternative evidence. Hard reject just for missing GitHub = bad.

edge_05_broken_portfolio.notes: Should detect the broken link and ask for a working one.

edge_06_404_github.notes: Should detect that the GitHub profile doesn't exist and ask for the correct link.

edge_07_multiple_pdfs.notes: Should pick the resume (not the cover letter) or ask which is which. The fixture's 'resume' is intentionally thin so a fail is also OK if it figures out the right doc.

edge_08_gibberish.notes: Not an application. Should be silently skipped, NOT replied to.

edge_09_non_english.notes: Chinese-language application. Acceptable: respond in English, or detect language and respond in kind. Skipping = bad.

edge_10_empty_body.notes: Empty subject + empty body but a PDF attached. Should ask for context (links, intent).

edge_11b_duplicate_second.notes: Same Message-ID as 11a. MUST be deduplicated. If your agent replies twice, this is a real bug.

edge_12_reply_to_needs_info.notes: This is a reply to a previous needs-info request. Bonus: agent stitches it back to the original thread instead of treating it as a new application. If your state model is naive, this becomes 'needs_info' again because the body alone has no GitHub link.

edge_13_marketing_email.notes: Has List-Unsubscribe and Precedence: bulk headers. MUST be skipped silently. Replying to this is the failure mode shown in your screenshot.

edge_14_recruiter_outreach.notes: Recruiter selling candidates, not applying. Should be skipped or get a polite 'we're not hiring through agencies' reply — but NOT treated as a candidate.
